title: 怪屋
summary:
After her brief display of terror, Margarita Leonidis adopts a business-like demeanor and discusses her performance of "Edith Thompson" with the narrator. She suggests providing specific details to the inspector, but her husband, Philip Leonidis, insists that he will manage all communications. Sophia Leonidis interrupts, instructing her mother to adopt a specific persona for the police: appearing pitiful, saying very little, and acting to protect the family. Sophia declares herself the "producer" of this performance.

Inspector Taverner arrives at the library to interview Philip. He informs Philip that Aristide did not die of natural causes but was killed by an overdose of belladonna (atropine), likely because the victim mistook eye drops for insulin. Taverner notes the suspicious absence of fingerprints on the empty eyedrop bottle found in the trash. Philip suggests the servant, Johnson, might be the culprit, but Taverner dismisses this, noting Johnson's loyalty and the substantial annual bonuses the deceased provided him.

During the interrogation, Philip states he spent the day in the library and only saw his father after breakfast, when Margarita was also present. Philip explains that Roger informed him of his father's sudden worsening condition. Philip called a doctor, but his father died before the doctor arrived. Regarding the family's finances, Philip explains that while Aristide had made the children financially independent through various assets and companies, the deceased remained a very wealthy man. Philip denies that his move to the house was due to financial difficulties, claiming it was for personal reasons and convenience.
